operation: First, a 2D sketch is constructed: draw a 15.175 mm radius outer circle, then subtract a 11.175 mm radius concentric inner circle to form the central annular profile. Two points are positioned at coordinates (17.5, 0) and (-17.5, 0) on the sketch X-axis, a 4 mm radius circle is drawn at each point, then a 2 mm radius concentric inner circle is subtracted from each to form the through-hole profiles for the side lugs. 1 mm fillets are applied to the topmost and bottommost vertices at the junctions between the main ring and the side lugs, followed by a sketch clean operation. The finished sketch is extruded 7 mm along the Z axis to create the 3D solid, then a 0.5 mm chamfer is added to all edges on the top and bottom faces perpendicular to the Z axis.
geometry: This is a flat, symmetric thick annular part, with two identical protruding lugs positioned on opposite sides along a shared central horizontal axis. Each lug has a concentric through-hole running the full height of the component. The transition corners between the central main ring and the side lugs are smoothly rounded, and all sharp outer edges on the top and bottom faces of the entire part have a small chamfer.
function: This is a symmetric mounting flange clamp designed to fit around a cylindrical shaft or component, and be fastened to a mating surface via the two side through holes to securely hold the cylindrical part in place for mechanical assembly use.
